title: The Case for a ‘Black Panther’ Best Picture Win, Plus a Conversation With Nominee Ruth E. Carter
format: Rob Harvilla and Micah Peters make the case for a 'Black Panther' Best Picture win, then costume designer Ruth E. Carter walks through building Wakanda for her Oscar-nominated work on the film.

segments.heading: The Case for ‘Black Panther’
segments.note: with Rob Harvilla and Micah Peters

films.title: Black Panther
films.note: Harvilla calls it the most complete of the seven Best Picture nominees; Peters, who saw it four times, traces his own arc from loving to hating to loving Michael B. Jordan's Killmonger. Both land on the ancestral-plane scene in Killmonger's Oakland apartment as the moment the film's mix of myth and real history clicked as Best Picture-worthy, even as they agree the CGI train fight at the end is the film's weak point.

films.title: Black Panther
films.note: Carter, nominated for Best Costume Design, describes pitching herself to Marvel without a script, building the panther suit thin enough for the stunt team (and hiring a Boston Ballet dancer to fix the gussets), and using Dogon masks and African weaponry as reference for the Jabari armor. Discusses her long collaboration with Ryan Coogler and, before this film, twelve movies with Spike Lee.

films.title: Mary Queen of Scots
films.note: Carter's answer to Sean's closing question — the last great movie she'd seen, for its makeup, hair, and clothes.

referenced.group: In passing during the Black Panther discussion
films: The Lord of the Rings: The Return of the King
